story_prompt: create a lecture on javascript function

scenes.text: In this first scene, we introduce the concept of a JavaScript function. A function is a block of code designed to perform a particular task, which is executed when it is invoked or called.
scenes.image_prompt: flowchart TD; A[Start] --> B[Define Function]; B --> C[Invoke Function]; C --> D[End];

scenes.text: Next, we discuss how to declare a function. In JavaScript, functions can be declared using the function keyword, followed by a name and a set of parentheses.
scenes.image_prompt: flowchart TD; A[Start] --> B[Declare Function using: function functionName() { }]; B --> C[Function Body]; C --> D[End];

scenes.text: Now, let's explore function expressions - a way to define a function using a variable. Functions can be stored in variables and passed around like other variables.
scenes.image_prompt: flowchart TD; A[Start] --> B[Define Function: const myFunction = function() { }]; B --> C[Invoke myFunction]; C --> D[End];

scenes.text: This scene covers arrow functions, a concise way to write functions in JavaScript, especially helpful for non-method functions in modern JavaScript.
scenes.image_prompt: flowchart TD; A[Start] --> B[Define Function using: const myFunction = () => { }]; B --> C[Invoke myFunction]; C --> D[End];

scenes.text: Finally, let's discuss parameters and arguments. Functions can take inputs known as parameters, which are specified within the parentheses during function declaration.
scenes.image_prompt: flowchart TD; A[Start] --> B[Define Function with Parameters: function add(a, b) { return a + b; }]; B --> C[Call add(2, 3)]; C --> D[Returns 5];
